title: Body pulled from Wakefield lake believed to be that of missing man
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The body recovered from Lake Quannapowitt is believed to be Jason Thomas, missing since December.
Jason Thomas, 45, was last seen near Murray and Chestnut streets, Wakefield, on December 12.
Authorities do not suspect foul play; the Medical Examiner will determine identity and cause of death.

title: Massachusetts online casino bill sent to study, effectively shelved for now
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts' bill H. 4431 to legalize online casino gambling was sent to study, stalling its progress this year.
The Joint Committee on Economic Development and Emerging Technologies voted 11-0, halting the bill sponsored by Rep. David Muradian.
If passed, the bill would have allowed state casinos to launch internet gaming platforms with a 15% state tax on revenues.

title: Police: Bus driver in Medford crash may have been asleep at the wheel
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: MBTA bus driver may have been drowsy before crashing in Medford on March 8, says Transit Police.
Driver, 40, faces charges for negligent operation, red light and lane violations, but not intoxication.
Crash involved the MBTA bus, several parked cars, and two buildings; no icy conditions were reported.

title: Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Black Mountain in Jackson, N.H. will not become a community co-op; Erik Mogensen purchased it to address challenges for independent ski areas.
Mogensen, CEO of Entabeni Systems, plans to use Black Mountain as a laboratory for generating solutions for struggling ski areas.
Mogensen's decision to redirect plans was supported by shareholders, and company headquarters will move to Jackson, N.H.

title: Why a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor is running the Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Julia Aldrich, a 35-year-old from Cincinnati, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Diagnosed with breast cancer at 32, Aldrich returned to racing eight weeks after her double mastectomy.
Aldrich's participation marks her first time running a marathon for charity, supporting cancer research and care initiatives.

title: An expert explains why the recent Danvers murder stands out
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, allegedly killed Janet Swallows, 68, in a random attack at her home in Danvers.
Criminologist James Alan Fox highlights teen murders as often lacking logical motives, unlike those committed by adults.
DeMayo, held without bail, pleaded not guilty; experts note teenage brain development impacts decision-making ability.

title: MGB researchers just developed a new risk calculator for obesity. Here’s why that’s a game changer.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mass General Brigham developed a genetic risk calculator predicting obesity-related health risks using data from over 8.5 million participants.
The calculator uses a polygenic risk score incorporating 20 metabolic traits, enabling early disease prediction across diverse populations.
High risk scores predicted higher likelihood of interventions like GLP-1 medications or bariatric surgery, emphasizing early prevention.

title: Why this ICU nurse is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support trauma recovery
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Erin Daniel, a 25-year-old ICU nurse at Brigham and Women's Hospital, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Daniel is part of the Stepping Strong team, supporting trauma recovery through advancing research and patient care.
Her inspiration comes from Gillian Reny's story and the mission of the Stepping Strong Foundation to improve patient outcomes.

title: Poll: Mass. Legislature underwater in court of public opinion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A poll shows only 28% of Massachusetts residents view the Legislature favorably, with 51% holding an unfavorable opinion.
87% of respondents view government transparency as crucial, and 86% support applying public records law to state lawmakers.
83% believe the Legislature should allow an audit by State Auditor Diana DiZoglio, which has been resisted so far.

title: Police seek Mass. vehicle after driver involved in fatal Providence hit-and-run
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Providence police seek a black Chevrolet Equinox with a Massachusetts plate involved in a fatal hit-and-run.
Kevin Chappie, 46, died after being struck while waiting for a rideshare near Corliss Street Saturday morning.
The public is urged to contact Providence Police with any information on the vehicle's whereabouts.
